meanings.definition: move something or somebody to a lower position
examples: take down the vase from the shelf
turn down the volume of a radio
The lack of rain had depressed the water level in the reservoir

meanings.definition: look angry or sullen, wrinkle one's forehead, as if to signal disapproval
less than normal in degree or intensity or amount
literal meanings; being at or having a relatively small elevation or upward extension

examples: the low-toned murmur of the surf

meanings.definition: used of sounds and voices; low in pitch or frequency

examples: his miserable treatment of his family

meanings.definition: low or inferior in station or quality
examples: a modest man of the people

meanings.definition: subdued or brought low in condition or status

examples: gloomy at the thought of what he had to face
took a grim view of the economy
lonely and blue in a strange city
depressed by the loss of his job
a dispirited and resigned expression on her face
